prediction_reason.历年位次与招生计划: 2021–2025年理科/物理类最低位次分别约12296→12840→14112→21997→23036名。2024年黑龙江省实施新高考改革后，物理类位次大幅后移（较2023年理科后移约7885名），2025年继续小幅后移至23036名，近两年累计后移约8924名；2025年基准位次约23036名；该校在黑龙江省理科/物理类招生计划2021–2024年均为108人，2025年略减至107人；2026年物理类计划大幅增至131人，较2025年扩招约22.4%，扩招幅度显著，将直接拉低竞争门槛，使最低录取位次后移。
prediction_reason.学校层次与声誉: 太原理工大学为211工程、双一流建设高校；软科中国大学主榜排名从2021年第113位持续上升至2026年第97位，排名稳步提升，反映出学校综合实力和认可度在增强，对考生吸引力有所提升；该校在2022年高考周期内声誉评价为偏负面（郑强书记管理受学生批评），但近年无重大负面舆情事件；抖音综合热度排名第63位，评价为"一般"，短视频平台曝光量处于中等水平，未出现明显报考关注升温信号；历史投档异常记录中未见该校身影；第五轮学科评估中，化学工程与技术获评A-，为该校唯一A-及以上学科；该校以工科为主，化工、机械、电气、计算机等工科专业群实力较为均衡，对理科考生有一定吸引力。
prediction_reason.专业热度与地域: 计算机类、电气工程及其自动化、电子信息/通信等工科专业社会热度持续偏高（2024年电气工程及其自动化获评"高热推荐"），与太原理工大学优势工科方向高度契合，有利于维持报考热度；黑龙江省2026年普通高考总人数约167000人，按近年物理类占比约68.2%估算，物理类考生约113894人，较2025年物理类117407人减少约3513人（约-3%），考生总数小幅下降使竞争略有缓和。；太原理工大学位于山西省太原市，非黑龙江省本地高校，属于跨省报考；该校在黑龙江招生计划占比较小（近年约占总招生计划的2%-3%），属于省外招生规模中等的高校，地域偏好对报考影响有限。
prediction_reason.预测结论: 综合2026年物理类招生计划大幅扩招约22.4%、软科排名持续上升至第97位（学校层次提升对考生吸引力增强）、黑龙江省物理类考生总数小幅减少约3%（竞争略有缓和）等因素，考虑软科排名上升带来的竞争加剧效应，小幅前移200名；预测2026年物理类最低录取位次约25936名，区间25436–26436名。

历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plan
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum rankings for Science/Physics Category were approximately 12296 → 12840 → 14112 → 21997 → 23036. After Heilongjiang Province implemented the new college entrance examination reform in 2024, the Physics Category ranking dropped significantly (about 7885 places compared to 2023 Science), and continued to drop slightly to 23036 in 2025, with a cumulative drop of about 8924 places over the past two years; the baseline ranking for 2025 is about 23036; the university's enrollment plan in Heilongjiang for Science/Physics Category was 108 students from 2021 to 2024, slightly reduced to 107 in 2025; in 2026, the Physics Category plan significantly increased to 131 students, an expansion of about 22.4% compared to 2025, which is a notable increase that will directly lower the competition threshold, causing the minimum admission ranking to drop.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: Taiyuan University of Technology is a 211 Project and Double First-Class construction university; its ranking in the Soft Science Chinese University Main Ranking has steadily risen from 113th in 2021 to 97th in 2026, reflecting an enhancement in the university's comprehensive strength and recognition, which increases its appeal to candidates; during the 2022 college entrance examination cycle, the university's reputation was somewhat negative (Secretary Zheng Qiang's management was criticized by students), but there have been no major negative public incidents in recent years; its comprehensive popularity ranking on Douyin is 63rd, rated as 'average', indicating moderate exposure on short video platforms without significant signs of increased application interest; no historical abnormal admission records were found for this university; in the fifth round of discipline evaluation, Chemical Engineering and Technology received an A-, the only A- or above discipline at this university; the university is primarily engineering-oriented, with a balanced strength in engineering disciplines such as chemical engineering, mechanical engineering, electrical engineering, and computer science, which has some appeal to science candidates.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: Engineering majors such as Computer Science, Electrical Engineering and Automation, and Electronic Information/Communication have consistently high social popularity (in 2024, Electrical Engineering and Automation was rated as 'highly recommended'), which aligns well with Taiyuan University of Technology's advantageous engineering directions, helping to maintain application enthusiasm; the total number of candidates for the 2026 Heilongjiang Province general college entrance examination is about 167,000, and based on the recent Physics Category proportion of about 68.2%, the estimated number of Physics Category candidates is about 113,894, a decrease of about 3,513 (approximately -3%) compared to 117,407 in 2025, slightly easing competition; Taiyuan University of Technology is located in Taiyuan City, Shanxi Province, not a local university in Heilongjiang, so it is a cross-provincial application; the university's enrollment plan in Heilongjiang accounts for a small proportion (about 2%-3% of the total enrollment plan in recent years), making it a medium-scale out-of-province enrollment university, with limited impact from regional preference on applications.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering factors such as the significant expansion of the 2026 Physics Category enrollment plan by about 22.4%, the continuous rise in Soft Science ranking to 97th (enhanced university level increasing candidate appeal), and a slight decrease of about 3% in the total number of Physics Category candidates in Heilongjiang (slightly easing competition), and taking into account the increased competition effect from the rising Soft Science ranking, a slight forward shift of 200 places is applied; the predicted minimum admission ranking for the 2026 Physics Category is about 25,936, with a range of 25,436 to 26,436.

major_tier_recommendation_en.overall_advice: In 2026, Taiyuan University of Technology significantly expands its Physics Category enrollment in Heilongjiang by about 22%, with a predicted minimum ranking of about 25,936, a drop of about 2,900 places compared to 2025, significantly lowering the admission threshold. Candidates with rankings within the predicted range of 25,436 to 26,436 are advised to primarily apply for Tier 2 majors, use Tier 1 flagship majors (Electrical Experimental Class, Computer Science, etc.) as reach options, and Tier 3 traditional engineering majors as safety choices; candidates with rankings significantly better than the range (e.g., within 24,000) can focus on Tier 1 majors; candidates with rankings near the upper limit of the range should primarily apply for Tier 3 majors to ensure admission.

历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plan
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum rankings for liberal arts/history category were approximately 2174 → 2148 → 3133 → 2556 → 2735. In 2023, the ranking significantly dropped to 3133 (about 985 places lower than 2022). After the new college entrance examination reform in 2024, the history category ranking rebounded to 2556, and in 2025 it slightly dropped to 2735. Over the past 5 years, the overall fluctuation range is about 2148–3133, with a baseline ranking of about 2735 in 2025. The university's enrollment plan for liberal arts/history category in Heilongjiang Province was 12 students from 2021 to 2024, reduced to 10 in 2025. The 2026 enrollment plan document does not include liberal arts/history category data, so it is treated as unchanged. In recent years, the liberal arts plan has shown a slight reduction trend, which intensifies competition to some extent.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: Taiyuan University of Technology is a 211 Project and Double First-Class university. Its ranking on the Soft Science main list has steadily risen from 113th in 2021 to 97th in 2026, indicating improving overall strength and recognition. In 2022, its reputation evaluation was slightly negative (Secretary Zheng Qiang's management was criticized by students), but there have been no major negative public opinions in recent years. Its Douyin popularity ranks 63rd, with an evaluation of 'average', showing no abnormal increase in application attention. There is no record of historical admission cut-off gaps. In the fifth round of discipline evaluation, only Chemical Engineering and Technology received an A- rating, and no liberal arts-related disciplines achieved A-level ratings. Taiyuan University of Technology is primarily engineering-oriented, with few liberal arts majors (only 4–5, including Administrative Management, Teaching Chinese to Speakers of Other Languages, Ideological and Political Education, and International Economics and Trade), and its overall liberal arts strength is average.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: Liberal arts-related majors (such as Law with divided outlook, Chinese Language/Literature recommended, Teacher Training/Education with divided outlook) have moderate to positive social popularity. In 2026, the total number of regular college entrance examination takers in Heilongjiang Province is about 167,000. Based on the recent history category proportion of about 31.8%, the estimated number of history category examinees is about 53,106, a decrease of about 2,601 (about -4.8%) compared to 54,707 in 2025. The slight decrease in total examinees eases competition to some extent. Taiyuan University of Technology is located in Shanxi Province, not a local university in Heilongjiang, so it is a cross-provincial application. The university's enrollment scale in Heilongjiang is small (10–12 liberal arts students in recent years), and regional preference has limited impact on applications.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the slight reduction in liberal arts enrollment plans in recent years, the continuous rise in Soft Science ranking (enhanced university level attracting more applicants), and the slight decrease of about 4.8% in the total number of history category examinees in Heilongjiang Province (easing competition), and factoring in the competition-easing effect from the decrease in history category examinees, a slight downward adjustment of 30 places is made. The predicted minimum admission ranking for the history category in 2026 is about 2765, with a range of 2267–3263.

major_tier_recommendation_en.overall_advice: Taiyuan University of Technology has few liberal arts/history category enrollment majors in Heilongjiang (only 4–5), and the university is primarily engineering-oriented with average liberal arts strength. The predicted minimum ranking for the history category in 2026 is about 2765, a slight drop of about 30 places from 2025. Candidates with rankings within the predicted range of 2267–3263 are advised to focus on Tier 2 majors (Teaching Chinese to Speakers of Other Languages, International Economics and Trade) as main choices, and Tier 1 majors (Ideological and Political Education, Administrative Management) as reach options. Candidates with rankings significantly better than the range may consider Ideological and Political Education (advantageous for civil service exams).
